commands.review_summary:
The PR adds readback-confirmed recovery for completed write-tool timeout/abort errors in host and sandboxed write tools, adds regressions for warning behavior and path normalization, and adjusts one ACP test helper literal.

PR surface: Source +136, Tests +227. Total +363 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main sends unresolved mutating write/edit errors through the visible warning path, and the linked report describes completed file writes still surfacing Write/Edit failed warnings. I did not run the live Feishu or Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer action for real behavior proof, dirty branch resolution, and current-head validation; there is no narrow automated code repair indicated by this review.

target.title: fix(agents): suppress Write/Edit failed warning on response-timeout false-failure (#55424)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the false "Write failed" / "Edit failed" warning class without trusting argument-derived payload metadata. Instead of suppressing payload warnings when `lastToolError.fileTarget` exists, the write tool wrapper now treats a timeout/abort as successful only after reading the target file back and confirming it contains the requested content.

This keeps visible warnings for real failures, partial writes, non-file mutating timeouts, and no-op/pre-aborted calls where the file already matched before the write attempt.

## Changes

- Add write-tool timeout recovery beside the existing edit recovery path in `src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts`.
- Apply write recovery to host and sandboxed write tools in `src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts`.
- Remove the unsafe payload warning-policy suppression from the original PR approach.
- Add regressions for readback-confirmed write recovery, failed readback, pre-existing matching content, timeout-like errors, `@` paths, and `file://` paths.
- Simplify one ACP test helper literal that tripped current core lint after rebasing onto `main`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: response/ack timeout after a completed file write no longer surfaces as a failed write when disk readback proves the requested content was written; argument-derived `fileTarget` alone still does not suppress payload warnings.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout plus Blacksmith Testbox Linux check runner.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base origin/main
pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-edit-recovery.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
pnpm check:changed
```

Evidence after fix: autoreview clean with no accepted/actionable findings; focused Vitest passed 105 tests; `pnpm check:changed` passed in Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksm38cvze3h6fh00msyyg76a` / Actions run `26495719466`.

Observed result after fix: write recovery returns success only after post-error readback matches requested content and the pre-call content did not already match; payload warning tests still show timed-out `write` errors when `fileTarget` only proves the attempted path.

What was not tested: live Feishu/Telegram end-to-end delivery was not rerun; the fix is covered at the shared write-tool and payload construction layers used by those channels.

Closes #55424
